findings.summary: derive_shared only validates that the peer Diffie-Hellman public value is in the integer range [2, P-2]. For RFC 3526 group 14 with G = 2, valid public keys are in the order-Q subgroup and must also satisfy y^Q mod P == 1. The in-range value P-2 is outside that subgroup but is accepted; because P-2 == -G mod P, the returned hash reveals whether the server's reusable static private exponent is even or odd.
findings.evidence: Running the repro prints accepted_invalid_public=P-2, shows pow(P-2,Q,P)=P-1 rather than 1, and shows leaked_static_private_parity equals actual_static_private_parity.
